canonical_definition: Multiple-choice question answering benchmark based on TV shows, requiring multimodal reasoning across video, audio, and subtitles.
papers: RepLoRA: Reparameterizing Low-rank Adaptation via the Perspective of Mixture of Experts
Unifying Specialized Visual Encoders for Video Language Models

definitions.text: Multiple-choice question answering benchmark based on TV shows, requiring multimodal reasoning across video, audio, and subtitles.
definitions.paper: Unifying Specialized Visual Encoders for Video Language Models

relationships.paper: Unifying Specialized Visual Encoders for Video Language Models
relationships.evidence: We evaluate our model on a comprehensive suite of video understanding benchmarks, including the open-ended MSVD-QA (Xu et al., 2017), MSRVTT-QA (Xu et al., 2017), TGIF (Jang et al., 2017), and ActivityNet-QA (Yu et al., 2019), as well as the multiple-choice benchmarks NExT-QA (Xiao et al., 2021), VLEP (Lei et al., 2020), TVQA (Lei et al., 2018), and Perception Test (Pătrăucean et al., 2023).
